raw_consumer_statement: The company made a good faith effort to provide some of the information relevant to the complaint by sending a letter stating that a {$200.00} - {$300.00} amount was in dispute. The issue with the transactions disputed that underlie the complaint is that they attract criminal activity by a third party that will go into any property to remove related documents to prevent resolution and deter any further communications regarding the dispute. This repeatedly occurred before the card was reissued through another bank. I am asking this company to correspond regarding the matters that were left unresolved an of which they had reason to know through access to the documents that I submitted and to which they confirmed during early conversations with the company at the time of the transfer to the new bank. Unfortunately, I need assistance with the dates but can locate this information when I am provided access and can somewhat bring forth supporting documentation. Irrespective of such, I successfully brought forth the matter twice in writing and in its entirety to the institution before the transfer and the new institution confirmed what I had reported. Criminal activities include sabotage to electronic recordkeeping of the transactions, such as through XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX. I have never dropped the matter from discussion. They simply stop corresponding and ask me to wait until I receive further assistance. Such even occurred in that manner when the institutions were changed.
